name: Taiwan (TSMC)
summary: Day 49. TAIEX 36,946 (+0.6%, +10.8% 1M, +17.6% 3M). TSMC Q1 2026 EARNINGS TODAY (April 16, 14:00 Taiwan / 02:00 ET). Revenue guide $34.6-35.8B (+38% YoY midpoint) = single most important tech-sector earnings print of Q1 2026 season. Beat + raised guide extends AI capex thesis (SMH +15% MoM already pricing). Miss triggers broad-based semi selloff (KOSPI/TAIEX -2-3%, SMH -4-6%). INDEPENDENT of Iran — this is the binary. Semiconductor strength most durable non-war driver.

signals.content: TAIEX at 33,113 (-0.7% daily, -6.5% monthly). TSMC has not publicly commented on helium supply impact but industry sources report allocation tightening. SMH (semiconductor ETF) down 9.2% monthly. If helium shortage forces TSMC to reduce advanced node production, cascade hits AI infrastructure (Nvidia, AMD, cloud providers) and tech valuations globally. (Sources: yfinance, gasworld, industry analysis)

TAIEX fell 2.2% (Mar 31 live) then rallied ~2.5% on April 1 peace signal. Taiwan is MORE HELIUM-DIVERSIFIED than South Korea: only ~30% from Qatar vs Korea's 64.7%. Rest split US (~30%) + other countries (~40%). This diversification provides meaningful buffer. Air Liquide commissioned new Taiwan factory — described as 'incremental, not transformative' by TrendForce. TSMC's helium exposure is moderate vs Samsung/SK hynix's high exposure. If helium crisis deepens, TSMC could gain relative advantage over Korean fabs — a competitive dynamic worth watching. (Sources: TrendForce, Digitimes, yfinance, Tom's Hardware)

Digitimes (April 1) reports Korean AND Taiwanese chipmakers diversifying helium sourcing beyond Qatar. Taiwan is more diversified than Korea: ~30% Qatar, ~30% US, ~40% others. Air Liquide new Taiwan factory 'incremental, not transformative' per TrendForce. TSMC's moderate helium exposure = competitive advantage vs Samsung/SK hynix if crisis deepens. TAIEX data from script shows recovery trend. (Sources: Digitimes, TrendForce, Tom's Hardware)

TAIEX at 32,572 (-1.8% 1D, -0.8% monthly). Taiwan less exposed than Korea to helium crisis — TSMC has diversified helium sources (~30% Qatar, ~30% US, ~40% others vs Korea's 64.7% Qatar dependency). But semiconductor supply chain stress from Korean fab allocations could cascade to TSMC if Samsung/SK hynix cut production — customers would redirect orders to TSMC, potentially straining its own helium-constrained capacity. TrendForce update window April 7-10 will provide the next data point on fab allocation status. KOSPI crashed -4.47% on April 3 (Samsung -5.9%, SK hynix -6.8%) — if Korean fabs announce production cuts, TSMC becomes both beneficiary (market share) and victim (helium strain from order surge). SMH semiconductor ETF at $392.32 (+0.1% 1D, -1.7% monthly) — broader semi sector relatively resilient.

Taiwan TAIEX +1.6% 1D to 33,097.37 — strong move; semis bid as helium cliff fails to materialize. TSMC has not announced production reduction. The helium fear premium is being unwound across Asian semis (KOSPI recovered, SMH +0.9%/+0.2% 1M). TrendForce April 7-10 production cut window opened with NO formal announcements. By April 10 if no announcement lands, the 'helium cliff' narrative weakens substantially.

TAIWAN TAIEX +6.4% TO 34,666 — DOUBLE CATALYST WITH KOSPI [CONFIRMED]. Taiwan TAIEX surged +6.4% on the ceasefire announcement, mirroring KOSPI's +7.9% / Samsung +13% / SK Hynix +11% rally. TSMC is the dominant index component and the proximate driver. Taiwan is less exposed than Korea to the helium crisis — TSMC has diversified helium sources (~30% Qatar dependency vs Korea's 65%) and the Samsung HeRS reuse system precedent applies to TSMC operations as well. The double-catalyst attribution: ~50% broad ceasefire risk-on rotation, ~25% the AI semiconductor cycle continuing (NVIDIA HBM3E demand from Samsung/SK Hynix indirectly benefits TSMC's leading-edge node capacity utilization), ~25% TSMC-specific China-Taiwan tension premium reduction (lower oil prices reduce China's import costs and ease the 'peak China' narrative). USD strength reversal also helps Taiwanese exporters. Taiwan has been the second-best performing major index of the war (+14.2% 3M) — only KOSPI has outperformed (+29.1%) due to the helium-relief specificity. **For the investor, Taiwan is the cleanest pure-AI semiconductor exposure that doesn't carry the same single-name (Samsung/SK Hynix) concentration risk as KOSPI.** The Friday Islamabad talks failure scenario reverses ~50% of today's TAIEX move; the AI semi cycle component is sticky.

TAIEX +4.5% TO 34,718 — DAY 2 OF SEMICONDUCTOR SUPPLY-CHAIN RELIEF [CONFIRMED]. Taiwan TAIEX continued the ceasefire-day rally with another +4.5% on Day 2, alongside KOSPI +5.6%, Nikkei +4.8%, SMH (US semiconductor ETF) +5.8%. The driver is helium supply chain normalization hopes — the TrendForce April 7-10 production-cut window has now passed Day 2 without any Samsung/SK Hynix announcement, meaning the helium-cliff bear case is functionally dead by April 10. TSMC is the dominant index component and benefits from the broader AI memory cycle continuing (NVIDIA HBM3E demand). Crisis-linked attribution ~65% (helium supply chain + ceasefire risk-on); ~35% structural AI cycle. Taiwan's relative position: less helium-exposed than Korea (~30% Qatar vs 65%) but indirectly benefits from Samsung/SK Hynix capacity coming back online. Second-best performing major index of the war at +14.6% 3M.

TAIEX +1.3% (35,328) — semiconductor relief continues. Samsung/SK Hynix signed 3-5yr helium LTAs with Linde and Air Products, killing the helium cliff bear case. TSMC's competitive position strengthened: TSMC is MORE diversified on helium (30% Qatar, 30% US, 40% other) than Korean rivals (64.7% Qatar per TrendForce). As Korean fabs locked in higher-cost LTAs to secure supply, TSMC's existing diversification becomes a cost advantage. The helium supply chain is structurally adapting — TSMC benefits from having adapted earlier. (Source: TrendForce, yfinance)

TAIEX +1.8% to 36,936. TSMC smashed closing record at NT$2,055 (+3.27%) — Q1 revenue +35% on AI chip demand. CoWoS packaging sold out through mid-2026. TSMC = 40%+ of TAIEX market cap, dragging TAIEX to record. MediaTek +6.17%, Hon Hai +3.75%. AI demand is REAL and independent of the war — the only war connection was helium supply risk, which has been largely resolved. Semiconductor strength is the most durable non-war driver in markets today. (Sources: yfinance, TSMC quarterly data)

EVENING UPDATE: TAIEX closed +1.2% — ASML guidance raise (EUR36-40B from EUR35-39B) confirms AI fab spending acceleration, validating TSMC's CoWoS packaging sold-out status through mid-2026. ASML's confidence in sustained chip equipment demand directly benefits TSMC as the dominant advanced node foundry. TSMC at record NT$2,055, Q1 revenue +35% — the AI semiconductor cycle is the most durable non-war driver in global markets. TAIEX = cleanest pure-AI semiconductor exposure without single-name concentration risk of KOSPI. (Sources: yfinance, ASML earnings data)

edges.context: TSMC uses helium as process gas and coolant in advanced chip fabrication. Qatar helium disruption threatens production capacity at world's most critical semiconductor facility.
TSMC supplies chips to Apple, Nvidia, AMD, Qualcomm — the largest S&P 500 components by market cap. Production disruption cascades directly to US tech valuations.
TSMC and Samsung compete in advanced semiconductor fabrication. Both exposed to same helium supply chain from Qatar.

trigger_points.condition: TSMC announces production reduction or allocation changes due to helium/gas supply
trigger_points.mechanism: TSMC producing 90% of advanced chips means any production cut cascades immediately to every tech company worldwide. This would shift the crisis from 'energy shock' to 'tech supply crisis.'
